Off Calendar Dec-16-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Max Ambrose, Esq., TAYLOR-COPELAND LAW 619-734-8770 maxambrose@taylorcopelandlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff JOHN CRESS
No other appearances

Plaintiff counsel appears out of an abundance of caution. Matter is not ARGUED/CONTESTED.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MIGUEL YNCLINO'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Cross-Defendant'S Special Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint;

"Defendant's motion for reconsideration of order granting cross-defendant's special motion to strike cross-complaint" [anti-SLAPP] is denied. A reconsideration motion must be "based on new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (CCP 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) Defendant says a "March 10, 2023 email represents new evidence." (11/22/24 Memo.) But that email is 20 months old and defendant discussed it in his 10/29/24 anti-SLAPP memorandum. (See, e.g., id. at 2:4-7.) Defendant also argues that the court's anti-SLAPP order "creates new legal circumstances." (11/22/24 Memo. 4:7-11.) But that would be so in virtually every case where a court order is filed, eviscerating CCP 1008. None of the legal authority relied on by defendant's 11/22/24 memo is new.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
